steps.explanation: From the dot plot, set A has mean 10 \mu\text{F} and range 12 \mu\text{F}. Every capacitor in set B is 14 \mu\text{F} greater than the corresponding capacitor in set A.
Adding a constant to every data value shifts the mean by that constant: mean of B = 10 + 14 = 24 \mu\text{F}.
Range = maximum − minimum. Adding the same constant to every value increases both max and min by 14, so their difference stays the same: range of B = 12 \mu\text{F}.

• A: Mean 10 \mu\text{F}, range 12 \mu\text{F} — The mean is unchanged from set A, but adding 14 to every value must shift the mean up by 14. Eliminated.
  • B: Mean 10 \mu\text{F}, range 26 \mu\text{F} — Both values are wrong; the mean is unshifted and the range is inflated. Eliminated.
  • C: Mean 24 \mu\text{F}, range 12 \mu\text{F} — Mean shifts by 14 (10 + 14 = 24) and range is unchanged. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: Mean 24 \mu\text{F}, range 26 \mu\text{F} — Mean is correct but range incorrectly doubles the shift (12 + 14 = 26). Eliminated.
